title: transport: connection_manager reconnect loops forever with no termination condition
description: connect_with_retry() spawns connection_manager which loops unconditionally: the outer loop has no break condition other than in_tx.is_closed() and the chat-exited path. If the relay is permanently down (domain no longer exists, certificate expired, etc.), the connection_manager will retry every 60s forever for the lifetime of the process. This is the intended design for a background reconnect service. However: 1) There is no maximum retry count or absolute timeout, so a daemon process that cannot reach its relay runs forever using CPU and generating log noise with no operator notification mechanism beyond the logs. 2) If open_authenticated_ws fails during the PoW mining step (spawn_blocking panics), the panic is caught by the JoinHandle and returned as an Err from the .await, which is then treated as a normal connection failure and retried — the error is lost in the warn!() log. No new P0 here but worth filing as P1 for operational correctness: consider adding a max-retry-count or a notifying channel.
